title: Sarga 54
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, virtue and right conduct. The passage moves from “Having heard Rama's speech, Lakshmana, who knew the highest truth, spoke words with folded hands to Rama, who was radiant with splendor.” through “The noble and blameless Narada and Parvata, having instilled great fear in me, have indeed departed to the three worlds, swift as the wind.” through “Let fragrant flowers always be placed in them, surrounding the area for me to a distance of one and a half Yojanas.”

Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, virtue and right conduct. The passage moves from “Having made this arrangement, the king then instructed his son, "O son, ever devoted to righteousness, protect your subjects according to the…” through “One obtains only what is destined to be obtained, goes only where one is destined to go, and receives only the sorrows and joys that are…” through “Having thus just entered that well adorned with great jewels, the great-souled king then fulfilled the curse that had indeed been uttered with…”

verses.sanskrit: रामस्य भाषितं श्रुत्वा लक्ष्मणः परमार्थवित् । उवाच प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं राघवं दीप्ततेजसम् ।। 7.54.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Rama's speech, Lakshmana, who knew the highest truth, spoke words with folded hands to Rama, who was radiant with splendor.

verses.sanskrit: अल्पापराधे काकुत्स्थ द्विजाभ्यां शाप ईदृशः । महान्नृगस्य राजर्षेर्यमदण्ड इवापरः ।। 7.54.2 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, for a minor offense, such a severe curse was given by the two Brahmins, like another punishment from Yama for the royal sage Nṛga.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु पापसंयुक्तमात्मानं पुरषर्षभ । किमुवाच नृगो राजा द्विजौ क्रोधसमन्वितौ ।। 7.54.3 ।।
verses.translation: O best of men, what did King Nṛga say to the two enraged Brahmins after hearing himself accused of sin?

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणेनैवमुक्तस्तु राघवः पुनरब्रवीत् । शृणु सौम्य यथा पूर्वं स राजा शापविक्षतः ।। 7.54.4 ।।
verses.translation: When Lakshmana thus spoke, Rama again said, "O gentle one, listen to how that king was previously afflicted by the curse."

verses.sanskrit: अथाध्वनि गतौ विप्रौ विज्ञाय स नृगस्तदा । आहूय मन्त्रिणः सर्वान्नैगमान्सपुरोधसः ।। 7.54.5 ।।
verses.translation: Then, having understood that the two Brahmins had departed, King Nṛga summoned all his ministers, citizens, and priests.

verses.sanskrit: तानुवाच नृगो राजा सर्वाश्च प्रकृतीस्तथा । दुःखेन सुसमाविष्टः श्रूयतां मे समाहितैः ।। 7.54.6 ।।
verses.translation: Deeply afflicted by sorrow, King Nṛga spoke to all of them, including his subjects, saying, "Let my words be heard by all of you with attention."

verses.sanskrit: नारदः पर्वतश्चैव मम दत्त्वा महद्भयम् । गतौ त्रिभुवनं भद्रौ वायुभूतावनिन्दितौ ।। 7.54.7 ।।
verses.translation: The noble and blameless Narada and Parvata, having instilled great fear in me, have indeed departed to the three worlds, swift as the wind.

verses.sanskrit: कुमारो ऽयं वसुर्नाम स देवो ऽद्याभिषिच्यताम् । श्वभ्रं च यत्सुखस्पर्शं क्रियतां शिल्पिभिर्मम । यत्राहं सङ्क्षयिष्यामि शापं ब्राह्मणनिःसृतम् ।। 7.54.8 ।।
verses.translation: Let this boy named Vasu be consecrated as a god today, and let artisans construct for me a pleasant cave where I can exhaust the curse issued by a Brahmin.

verses.sanskrit: वर्षघ्नमेकं श्वभ्रं तु हिमघ्नमपरं तथा । ग्रीष्मघ्नं तु सुखस्पर्शमेकं कुर्वन्तु शिल्पिनः ।। 7.54.9 ।।
verses.translation: The artisans should construct one cave that is rain-proof, another that is cold-proof, and a third, pleasant to touch, that is summer-proof.

verses.sanskrit: फलवन्तश्च ये वृक्षाः पुष्पवत्यश्च या लताः । विरोप्यन्तां बहुविधाश्छायावन्तश्च गुल्मिनः ।। 7.54.10 ।।
verses.translation: Let fruit-bearing trees, flowering creepers, and various kinds of shade-giving shrubs be planted.

verses.sanskrit: क्रियतां रमणीयं च श्वभ्राणां सर्वतोदिशम् । सुखमत्र वसिष्यामि यावत्कालस्य पर्ययः ।। 7.54.11 ।।
verses.translation: Let the surroundings of the caves be made beautiful in all directions, for I will dwell there comfortably until the end of my destined time.

verses.sanskrit: पुष्पाणि च सुगन्धीनि क्रियतां तेषु नित्यशः । परिवार्य यथा मे स्युरध्यर्धं योजनं तथा ।। 7.54.12 ।।
verses.translation: Let fragrant flowers always be placed in them, surrounding the area for me to a distance of one and a half Yojanas.

verses.sanskrit: एवं कृत्वा विधानं स सन्दिदेश वसं तदा । धर्मनित्यः प्रजाः पुत्र क्षत्रधर्मेण पालय ।। 7.54.13 ।।
verses.translation: Having made this arrangement, the king then instructed his son, "O son, ever devoted to righteousness, protect your subjects according to the duty of a Kshatriya."

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्यक्षं ते यथा शापो द्विजाभ्यां मयि पातितः । नरश्रेष्ठ सरोषाभ्यामपराधे ऽपि तादृशे ।। 7.54.14 ।।
verses.translation: O best among men, you yourself witnessed how a curse was inflicted upon me by the two angry Brahmins, even for such an offense.

verses.sanskrit: मा कृथास्तनुसन्तापं मत्कृते ऽपि नरर्षभ । कृतान्तः कुशलः पुत्र येनास्मि व्यसनीकृतः ।। 7.54.15 ।।
verses.translation: O best of men, do not grieve for my sake, O son, for destiny is powerful, and it is by its force that I have been afflicted with this misery.

verses.sanskrit: प्राप्तव्यान्येव प्राप्नोति गन्तव्यान्येव गच्छति । लब्धव्यान्येव लभते दुःखानि च सुखानि च ।। 7.54.16 ।।
verses.translation: One obtains only what is destined to be obtained, goes only where one is destined to go, and receives only the sorrows and joys that are destined to be received.

verses.sanskrit: पूर्वे जात्यन्तरे वत्स मा विषादं कुरुष्व ह ।। 7.54.17 ।।
verses.translation: O son, do not grieve over what happened in a former birth.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा नृपस्तत्र सुतं राजा महायशाः । श्वभ्रं जगाम सुकृतं वासाय पुरुषर्षभ ।। 7.54.18 ।।
verses.translation: O best among men, having thus spoken to his son, the highly renowned king went into the well, which was well-constructed for his dwelling.

verses.sanskrit: एवं प्रविश्यैव नृपस्तदानीं श्वभ्रं महारत्नविभूषितं तत् । सम्पादयामास तदा महात्मा शापं द्विजाभ्यां हि रुषा विमुक्तम् ।। 7.54.19 ।।
verses.translation: Having thus just entered that well adorned with great jewels, the great-souled king then fulfilled the curse that had indeed been uttered with anger by the two Brahmins. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे चतुःपञ्चाशः सर्गः ।। 54 ।। Thus ends the fifty-fourth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
